step_by_step_reasoning: We identify 11 nodes from the whiteboard image and transcript. Ingest starts with IoT devices sending data to a Lambda function, which writes raw telemetry data into an S3 data lake. The raw data is then batched into an SQS queue. A second Lambda function processes messages from SQS, combining them with pre-chunked OpenStreetMaps (OSM) data stored in a separate S3 bucket. This enriched stream is sent to Kinesis for sliding window calculations of safety events (e.g., speeding). Detected events are stored in a middle S3 bucket. For analytics and reporting, AWS Glue aggregates data from both the raw S3 bucket (via Athena) and the enriched S3 bucket, transferring it down to on-premises data centers.

graph.name: LeasePlan OneConnect Telematics Platform
graph.notes: OneConnect ingests vehicle telematics data, batches it using SQS, enriches it with OpenStreetMap data via Lambda and Kinesis, and uses Glue to sync telemetry analytics to on-premises reporting systems.

nodes.name: IoT Devices
nodes.notes: On-vehicle sensors capturing GPS, speed, and g-forces.

nodes.name: SQS Queue
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Batches raw records to ensure processing durability and scale.

nodes.name: Enrichment Lambda
nodes.notes: Performs rapid lookup against pre-chunked map data.

nodes.name: OpenStreetMap S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Contains OSM data pre-divided into 1-degree coordinate chunks via Athena.

nodes.name: Kinesis Stream
nodes.notes: Performs sliding window analytics to identify safety violations (speeding, harsh braking).

nodes.name: Athena
nodes.notes: Runs ad-hoc queries over raw data for summary reports.

nodes.name: AWS Glue
nodes.notes: Orchestrates extraction of events and raw summary data.

nodes.name: On-Premises Data Center
nodes.notes: Receives processed telemetry to drive customer dashboards.

edges.notes: Syncs reporting data to on-prem databases.
